title: ranunculus cassubicus subsp. hannae
intro: Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. hannae, also known as the white buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. hannae include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
